events.sourceOriginal: According to Mwami Kahindo Nzanzu Bonane, chief of the Baswagha chiefdom

events.sideA: Government of United Kingdom, Government of United States of America

events.sourceOriginal: Secretariat of Public Security and Social Defense (SSPDS)
Ukraine (head of the Donetsk OVA, Vadym Filashkin)
Secretariat of Public Security and Social Defense (SSPDS)

events.title: CENTCOM: U.S. forces disabled the Iranian-flagged tankers Sea Star III and Sevda on May 8 as they attempted to enter an Iranian port in violation of the U.S. blockade. A U.S. Navy F/A-18 Super Hornet from USS George H.W. Bush struck both vessels’ smokestacks with precision munitions, preventing them from reaching Iran
Fox News, citing a US official: Airstrikes today targeted empty oil tankers attempting to breach the blockade on Iran. The targeted vessels were supertankers trying to return to Iran.
UAE Defense Ministry: Air defenses intercepted two ballistic missiles and three drones
Reports of a new attack on Bandar Abbas and Qeshm in southern Iran
Chinese Foreign Ministry: No reports of injuries, even among the crew of the Chinese tanker in the Strait of Hormuz.
The UAE Ministry of Defense announces that its air defenses intercepted missile and drone attacks coming from Iranian territory.
U.S. destroyers have made two Strait of Hormuz transits this week, both times under fire—this time U.S. forces hit back striking targets in Iran yesterday, including Qeshm Island and Bandar Abbas

President Trump tells in a phone call the retaliatory strikes against Iranian targets are just a "love tap." "It's just a love tap." When I asked if it means the ceasefire is over. "No, no, the ceasefire is going. It's in effect

The American military confirms carrying out "self-defense" strikes in Iran in response to "unprovoked Iranian attacks" against US Navy missile destroyers that transited the Strait of Hormuz to the Gulf of Oman. "Iranian forces launched multiple missiles, drones and small boats as USS Truxtun (DDG 103), USS Rafael Peralta (DDG 115), and USS Mason (DDG 87) transited the international sea passage," US Central Command says in a statement. CENTCOM says it intercepted the "inbound threats," and "no US
US military: Our forces carried out defensive strikes against Iranian military installations

US destroyers USS Truxtun and USS Mason came under a sustained Iranian attack in the Strait of Hormuz; fast-attack boats, drones and missiles. American forces fought back. No casualties or damage reported - CBS News

The IRGC Navy in and statement said US naval forces came under attack by Iranian forces while attempting to approach an Iranian oil tanker near Jask, claiming that American warships suffered heavy damage in the confrontation
Governor of Minab, southern Iran: The naval base in the province was targeted by the US
A US official confirmed to Fox News: We attacked an Iranian base in Bandar Qarghan in the Minab province.
Iran's joint military command says the U.S. carried out air strikes in civilian areas with cooperation of some regional countries

In a statement, Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters said the US with several regional countries, attacked an Iranian oil tanker in the Strait of Hormuz as well as the cities of Minab, Bandar Abbas, Khamir, Sirik and Qeshm. The statement said that Iranian responded immediately
US military just carried out strikes on Iran's Qeshm Port and Bandar Abbas: Senior US official tells, but this is NOT a restarting of the war
The UAE announces the establishment of a national committee to document Iranian acts of aggression, international crimes, and the resulting damages.
The Iranian president: I recently met with Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei for two and a half hours in an atmosphere of trust and dialogue.

Trump paused his plan to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after Saudi Arabia blocked the U.S. military from using its bases and airspace for the operation. The restriction cut off critical access needed for aircraft, refueling, and protection missions, forcing the White House to halt “Project Freedom” shortly after it began.
Trump on Iran: It will end "quickly".

The UAE condemns the Iranian Foreign Ministry's statement and affirms its categorical rejection of any claims or threats that infringe upon its sovereignty, national security, or independence of decision-making.
Trump to Fox News: Timeframe for an Iran deal is one week

A U.S. Navy F/A-18 Super Hornet fired on an Iranian-flagged vessel attempting to breach the naval blockade, according to CENTCOM. The tanker is "no longer transiting to Iran" after the rudder was hit by 20mm rounds fired from the jet's gatling gun
Iran to run out of onshore and offshore oil storage in 40-45 days, will begin closing oil wells, some closures will be permanent, an Iranian Oil Ministry official tells NYT

France's only aircraft carrier, The FS Charles de Gaulle has crossed the Suez Canal and is en route to enter the Red Sea. The French Ministry of Defense has stated that the carrier group will deploy to the Southern Red Sea/Gulf of Aden Area in preparation for a future freedom of navigation operation in the Strait of Hormuz
Trump told PBS: The agreement stipulates that Iran will export highly enriched uranium to the United States.
Trump tells PBS: Special envoys Steve Wittkoff and Jared Kushner unlikely to be sent to participate in talks

Iran says it's reviewing latest US proposal to end the war, according to the country's state-owned news agency @isna_farsi. The news agency however pours cold water on the Axios report, saying it contains "ambitious and unrealistic proposals
Iranian air defenses reportedly shot down a drone overnight near the Strait of Hormuz, close to Qeshm Island. Source: Fars
